inputs.label: Your name
inputs.description: Your name (for signing auto-replies). If not set, auto-replies will not be signed.

inputs.label: Auto-reply sender types
inputs.description: Sender types or patterns that should receive auto-replies, one per line

inputs.label: Label rules
inputs.description: Rules for organizing emails into labels/folders, one per line (format: pattern -> label)
inputs.placeholder:
invoices -> Finance
newsletters -> Newsletters
meeting -> Scheduling

inputs.label: Morning briefing time
inputs.description: When to deliver your morning briefing (24h format, weekdays only)

inputs.label: Evening wrap time
inputs.description: When to deliver your evening wrap-up (24h format, weekdays only)

email-watcher.description: Relay new email events into the EventBus for organize-emails, auto-reply, and handle-meeting-requests workflows

calendar-watcher.description: Relay calendar event changes into the EventBus for meeting prep and schedule conflict workflows

organize-emails.description: Organize emails into folders based on labels

steps: Read the email data from _event_payload (contains from, subject, body, labels from the gmail +watch NDJSON output)
Match sender/subject against the user's configured label_rules patterns from inputs
If no rule matches, infer category from sender domain and content keywords (e.g., 'invoice', 'newsletter', 'meeting request', 'notification')
Determine the target Gmail label for this email and extract the message ID from _event_payload for the next activity

activities.intent: Apply labels and move to appropriate folders in Gmail
steps: Using the message ID from prior results and the category determined by the categorize activity
Check if the target label exists using: gws gmail +labels
If the label doesn't exist, create it using: gws gmail users labels create --params '{"userId": "me"}' --json '{"name": "<label_name>"}'
Apply the label to the message using: gws gmail +manage to add the label ID to the message
If the email should leave the inbox (newsletters, notifications), remove the INBOX label to archive it

activities.intent: Analyze incoming email content and sender
steps: Read the email data from _event_payload (contains from, subject, body, messageId from the gmail +watch NDJSON output)
Identify the sender type and email pattern from the from/subject/body fields
Check against the user's configured auto_reply_senders list from inputs

activities.intent: Match email against known patterns and determine action
steps: If email matches known patterns (spam, newsletter, etc.), apply appropriate action
For spam: skip — do not reply
For newsletters: skip — no reply needed
For known sender types in auto_reply_senders: proceed to auto-reply
For unknown patterns: skip — do not auto-reply to unrecognized senders

activities.intent: Send auto-reply if configured for that sender type
steps: Generate an appropriate auto-reply based on the email content and sender type from prior results
Sign with the user's configured name from inputs
Send the reply using the message ID from _event_payload: gws gmail +reply --id <messageId from _event_payload> --body '<auto_response>'

handle-meeting-requests.description: Detect meeting requests in incoming emails and react to calendar event changes

activities.intent: Check if the email contains a meeting request with clear time and attendees
steps: Read the email data from _event_payload (contains from, subject, body from the gmail +watch NDJSON output)
Check if the subject or body contains a meeting request with explicit date/time and attendees
Only proceed if both time and attendees are clearly stated — do not guess or infer vague mentions
Extract: summary, start time, end time, attendee emails, location (if any)

activities.intent: Create the calendar event from the detected meeting request
steps: Check for conflicts using: gws calendar +agenda --today (or the relevant day)
If conflict detected, flag it to the user instead of silently overbooking
Create the event using: gws calendar +insert --summary '<subject>' --start '<start_time>' --end '<end_time>' --attendee '<attendee_email>'

morning-briefing.description: Morning briefing: overnight email summary, today's calendar, and action items

activities.intent: Get unread email summary since yesterday
steps: Run: gws gmail +triage --max 30 to get unread messages summary
Categorize: action required, FYI, scheduling, can wait
Flag emails from VIPs or with urgent language
Count totals: how many new, how many need attention

activities.intent: Pull today's calendar and flag anything that needs prep
steps: Run: gws calendar +agenda --today to get today's events
Identify meetings that need preparation (board meetings, client calls, interviews)
Note any conflicts or back-to-back blocks
Flag deadlines or deliverables due today

activities.intent: Compile email + calendar into a scannable morning briefing
steps: Lead with the single most important thing across email and calendar
Structure: What Needs Attention > Today's Schedule > Inbox Summary
Connect related items — a meeting request email plus a calendar conflict is one story
End with a clear action items list if any exist
Keep it scannable — the user reads this with coffee, not a magnifying glass

day-monitor.description: Watches for urgent emails and upcoming meetings — interrupts only when it matters

activities.intent: Fast check for urgent emails and imminent calendar events
steps: Run: gws gmail +triage --max 5 --query 'is:unread newer_than:30m' for recent unread
Check for urgent keywords (urgent, ASAP, immediate) in subjects
Run: gws calendar +agenda --today to check for meetings starting in the next 30 minutes
Only alert if: urgent email arrived, or a meeting needs attention in the next 30m
Silence is fine. Do NOT alert for routine updates. This is interrupt-driven.

evening-wrap.description: End-of-day wrap: what happened today, what's unresolved, what's tomorrow

activities.intent: Summarize the day's email and calendar activity
steps: Summarize what happened today: emails processed, meetings completed, any urgent items that fired
Flag anything from this morning's briefing that may not have been addressed
Check for unread emails that still need replies

activities.intent: Preview tomorrow's schedule so the user can prep tonight
steps: Run: gws calendar +agenda --tomorrow for tomorrow's events
Flag early meetings or anything that needs advance prep
Keep it short — the user is winding down, not ramping up

check-inbox.description: On-demand inbox check: triage unread, read details, and categorize

steps: Run: gws gmail +triage --max 10 --query 'is:unread' to get unread messages summary

activities.intent: Read each unread message to extract full details
steps: For each unread message, run: gws gmail +read --id <message_id> --headers to extract sender, subject, date, and body

activities.intent: Categorize each email based on content and sender
steps: Categorize email based on sender/domain or content keywords (e.g., 'newsletter', 'invoice', 'meeting request')

activities.intent: Flag and notify on urgent emails
steps: If urgent keywords detected ('urgent', 'ASAP', 'immediate'), send notification via: message(resource: notify, action: alert, title: "Urgent Email", text: "<sender>: <subject>")

activities.intent: Skip auto-reply for newsletters and low-priority emails
steps: If newsletter/low-priority, skip auto-reply and archive later

activities.intent: Draft standard replies for emails that need responses
steps: For standard replies needed, draft response using: gws gmail +reply --id <message_id> --body '<auto_response_template>' --draft
